title: Next Greater Node In Linked List
hints: Look at the output for each node: it is the first larger value to its right. This resembles finding the 'next greater element' in an array, but the input is a linked list.
Convert the linked list to an array first. Then, use a monotonic stack to efficiently find the next greater element for each index in a single pass.
The stack should store indices of elements whose next greater element is not yet found. When a larger element appears, it resolves the pending queries for smaller elements currently in the stack.
